terms.definition: An anonymous function defined inline with the lambda keyword, limited to a single expression.
A function that takes one or more functions as arguments and/or returns a function, e.g. map, filter, sorted and decorator factories.
A built-in that applies a function to every item of an iterable, returning a lazy iterator of results.
A built-in that keeps only the items of an iterable for which a predicate function returns true.
The key function passed to sorted()/list.sort() that maps each element to the value used for ordering.
A helper that returns a new callable with some arguments of an existing function pre-filled.
A functools function that folds an iterable into a single value by repeatedly applying a two-argument function.
